headline: Technique-level research methods led the month with agent skills, tokenization, and vulnerability detection all breaking out at peak momentum.
body: A cluster of training and evaluation techniques topped the rotation this window, with agent skills, tokenization, and vulnerability detection each at maximum momentum and ratio, alongside breakouts in Gradient Staleness, behavioral auditing, ablation, masking, vector search, and multi-view consistency. On the model side, Meituan released LongCat-2.0, a 1.6 trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts model that activates roughly 48 billion parameters per token and was trained on AI ASIC superpods rather than Nvidia GPUs. The agent integration theme stayed active, with OpenAI describing a plan to merge chat and agent interfaces into persistent agents, and Anthropic pursuing direct Microsoft Teams integration against Microsoft's Copilot. Vibe coding platform Base44 began rolling out its own model as startups chase defensibility, while commentary tracked a narrowing gap between open-source and frontier models.

cited_items.title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chat And Agents — With Greg Brockman
Meituan LongCat 2.0 (Tested): China's 1.6T OPEN MODEL looks CRAZY!
Anthropic vs. Microsoft: The AI Agent Integration Battle! #shorts
Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
AI Frontier vs Open Source: The Gap is CLOSING FAST! #shorts
